task_completion.description: Navigate to the Routes module in CAMEO Data Manager
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Data Manager application is open and the 'Routes' module or list view is visible on the screen.
The text 'Norfolk Southern - Heartland Corridor' is visible in the Route Name input field of a new or editing route record.
The option 'Rail' is selected or entered in the Type dropdown or input field.
The text 'Primary double-stack intermodal route running East-West through the central business district.' is visible in the Description text area.
The text 'High frequency transport of Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5) and Vinyl Chloride.' is visible in the Comments or Notes text area.
The record is saved, evidenced by a saved state view of the record, a success message, or the new route appearing in the main Routes list view.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual CAMEO Data Manager GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual CAMEO Data Manager application interface being used, rather than a text editor, web browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or database browsers to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows with SQLite commands, Python scripts, or database browser applications (like DB Browser for SQLite) are used to insert the record directly.

integrity.description: Data was entered into the correct form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The requested text is clearly placed inside the designated input fields of the CAMEO Data Manager route creation form.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Norfolk Southern Heartland Corridor is a real double-stack intermodal rail route. Chlorine's CAS number is indeed 7782-50-5. Note that while the real route primarily runs through Appalachian regions and mid-size cities, the task explicitly requires entering 'central business district' into the description. The VLM must verify that the exact text from the task description is entered into the CAMEO Data Manager GUI, as this is a synthetic test of GUI interaction.
